meaning: vt.发生,得知,使蒸发,使排出vi.蒸发,发散,泄露
examples: The plants transpire to give off more water vapor.
All memorable events, I should say, transpire in morning time and in a morning atmosphere.
For example, a cornfield 1 acre in size can transpire as much as 4,000 gallons of water every day.
What ultimately will transpire in Libya probably lies somewhere in between the extreme scenarios.
When someone is this eager and excited to excel, and is given the environment to thrive in, miracles transpire.
